subtitle: Microbes Must Have Parents!
summary: While Spallanzani is away on his famous voyage, jealous professors accuse him of stealing university specimens. He returns to a hero's welcome from his students and is cleared by an investigation. He then tackles a new question: how do microbes multiply? After learning of de Saussure's discovery that microbes split in two, Spallanzani devises an ingenious experiment to isolate a single animalcule and watches it divide, proving that microbes reproduce by splitting apart rather than giving birth like larger animals.

segments.anchor_sentence: While Spallanzani was on his triumphant voyage a dark cloud gathered away to the south, at his university, the school at Pavia that he had done so much to bring back to life.

visual.scene_description: Canon Volta sneaks into Spallanzani's private museum at Scandiano, discovering specimens with university tags, plotting his accusation.
visual.animation_prompt: Simple linocut print style illustration, bold black outlines, flat muted colors in dusty rose and sage green and ochre, rough textured edges, hand-printed look on natural paper, children's book illustration, a thin man in a black Catholic canon's cloak sneaking through a dim private museum room, examining glass specimen jars with red tags, stuffed birds on shelves, an evil grin on his face, candlelight casting long shadows across wooden cabinets filled with natural history specimens, 18th-century Italian interior

segments.anchor_sentence: He returned to Pavia and on his way there I wonder what his thoughts were—did he see himself slinking into the town, avoided by old admirers and a victim of malignant hissing whispers?

visual.scene_description: Spallanzani arrives at the gates of Pavia to find a jubilant mob of students rushing out to welcome him back and escort him to his lecture chair.
visual.animation_prompt: Simple linocut print style illustration, bold black outlines, flat muted colors in dusty rose and sage green and ochre, rough textured edges, hand-printed look on natural paper, children's book illustration, a tall thin sharp-featured man in a black priest's cassock approaching stone city gates, a crowd of young 18th-century students in tricorn hats and waistcoats rushing toward him with arms raised in celebration, cobblestone road leading to an Italian university town with arched gateway, bright open sky above

segments.anchor_sentence: Many times in his long years of looking at the animalcules he had wondered how they multiplied.

visual.scene_description: Spallanzani peers through his microscope at two animalcules stuck together, puzzling over whether they are mating or dividing, with a circular inset showing a rod-shaped microbe pinching in the middle.
visual.animation_prompt: Simple linocut print style illustration, bold black outlines, flat muted colors in dusty rose and sage green and ochre, rough textured edges, hand-printed look on natural paper, children's book illustration, a tall thin man in a black cassock hunched over a brass microscope at a wooden desk, notebook open with crude sketches, quill pen beside it, 18th-century Italian study with bookshelves, circular magnified inset showing two rod-shaped animalcules connected at their midpoints beginning to separate, translucent cell walls visible

segments.anchor_sentence: Then Spallanzani was forced into one of the most devilishly ingenious researches of his life.

visual.scene_description: Spallanzani sits alone in his laboratory, deep in thought, planning how to isolate a single microbe to prove Ellis wrong, with glass instruments and seed soup flasks around him.
visual.animation_prompt: Simple linocut print style illustration, bold black outlines, flat muted colors in dusty rose and sage green and ochre, rough textured edges, hand-printed look on natural paper, children's book illustration, a tall thin sharp-featured man in a black cassock sitting at a cluttered 18th-century laboratory bench, chin resting on hand in deep concentration, sealed glass flasks of cloudy seed soup arranged before him, fine needles and crystal glass pieces laid out, candlelight illuminating the scene, dramatic gesturing hand raised mid-thought

segments.anchor_sentence: He went to his laboratory and carefully put a drop of seed soup swarming with animalcules on a clean piece of crystal glass.

visual.scene_description: Spallanzani performs his ingenious isolation technique: using a fine needle to draw a canal between two drops on crystal glass, trapping a single animalcule in the clean water drop.
visual.animation_prompt: Simple linocut print style illustration, bold black outlines, flat muted colors in dusty rose and sage green and ochre, rough textured edges, hand-printed look on natural paper, children's book illustration, close-up of hands in dark sleeves holding a hair-fine needle over a piece of crystal glass bearing two tiny drops of liquid connected by a thin canal, a small camel-hair brush nearby, brass single-lens microscope positioned above, 18th-century Italian laboratory table with candle flame reflected in the glass surface

segments.anchor_sentence: Then a strange sight startled him, not less dramatic for its unbelievable littleness.

visual.scene_description: Through the microscope lens, a single rod-shaped animalcule pinches in the middle and splits into two identical creatures, shown in a large circular magnified inset.
visual.animation_prompt: Simple linocut print style illustration, bold black outlines, flat muted colors in dusty rose and sage green and ochre, rough textured edges, hand-printed look on natural paper, children's book illustration, a tall thin man in a black cassock peering intently through a brass microscope, large circular magnified inset dominating the composition showing a rod-shaped animalcule thinning at its center and splitting into two identical smaller rod-shaped creatures with visible cell walls, four daughter cells nearby, 18th-century laboratory background
